questions.text: Who was Tutankhamun?
options: The last heir of a powerful family that ruled Egypt
A priest of the god Amun
questions.explanation: Tutankhamun was a teenage pharaoh who was the last of his royal lineage to rule the Egyptian empire.

questions.text: When was Tut's tomb discovered?
questions.explanation: British archaeologist Howard Carter discovered Tut's tomb in 1922 after years of futile searching.

questions.text: Who discovered Tutankhamun's tomb?
questions.explanation: Howard Carter was the British archaeologist who famously found the tomb in the Valley of the Kings.

questions.text: What was found in the first of the three nested coffins?
options: A shroud adorned with garlands of willow and olive leaves
questions.explanation: The first coffin contained a shroud decorated with floral offerings, suggesting a burial in March or April.

questions.text: Why was it difficult to remove Tut's body from the coffin?
options: The ritual resins had hardened, cementing the body to the bottom
Archaeologists were afraid of the curse
questions.explanation: The resins used during the burial had solidified, making it impossible to lift the body out without force.

questions.text: How did Carter eventually remove the resins?
options: Using chisels to cut the body away
Leaving it in the sun for hours
questions.explanation: After failing to melt the resin in the hot sun, Carter's men had to use chisels to separate the mummy from the gold coffin.

questions.text: What did an anatomy professor discover about Tut in 1968?
options: His breastbone and front ribs were missing
questions.explanation: An X-ray taken in 1968 revealed that the king's breastbone and front ribs were missing, pointing towards a possible injury or foul play.

questions.text: What was the purpose of the CT scan performed in 2005?
options: To create a 3D image and solve the mystery of his death
To move the body to a new museum
questions.explanation: The CT scan aimed to provide precise data for a forensic reconstruction and investigate how he died.

questions.text: Which god was the 'King of the Gods' that Amenhotep IV attacked?
questions.explanation: Amenhotep IV (Akhenaten) shocked Egypt by attacking the major god Amun, smashing his images and closing his temples.

questions.text: What name did Amenhotep IV change his to?
questions.explanation: He changed his name to Akhenaten, meaning 'servant of the Aten' (the sun disk).

questions.text: Tutankhamun changed his name from Tutankhaten to show his devotion to:
questions.explanation: Tutankhamun means 'Living Image of Amun', signifying the restoration of the old ways.

questions.text: What was the 'Pharaoh's Curse' mentioned in the chapter?
options: Death or misfortune to those who disturb him
Eternal life to those who find him
A hidden trap in the tomb
questions.explanation: The curse warned that death or misfortune would fall upon those who disturbed the pharaoh's rest.

questions.text: How many years after his death was Tut's body scanned?
questions.explanation: Tut died more than 3,300 years ago, and his body was scanned in 2005.

questions.text: Who is Zahi Hawass?
options: The Secretary General of Egypt's Supreme Council of Antiquities
The person who funded the excavation
questions.explanation: Zahi Hawass was the prominent Egyptian archaeologist overseeing the CT scan of King Tut.

questions.text: What happened to the CT scanner during the process?
options: It stopped working because of sand in a cooler fan
questions.explanation: The scan was interrupted when the machine's fan failed due to sand, which a guard jokingly called 'the pharaoh's curse'.

questions.text: Which constellation was known as the soul of Osiris by ancient Egyptians?
questions.explanation: Orion, the constellation the Egyptians believed was the soul of Osiris (god of the afterlife), stood above the tomb.

questions.text: The burial chamber was located how deep underground?
questions.explanation: The rock-cut tomb was 26 feet underground.

questions.text: Tut died when he was a:
questions.explanation: Tutankhamun was a teenager when he died, after ruling for about nine years.

questions.text: Akhenaten moved the religious capital from Thebes to:
questions.explanation: Akhenaten moved the capital to the new city of Akhetaten, now known as Amarna.

questions.text: What was the 'scientific' reason the CT scan was considered successful?
options: It showed the body was still intact and revealed no current danger
questions.explanation: The scan showed that despite the rough handling by Carter, the mummy was stable and provided great detail of its structure.

questions.text: Tut ruled for about:
questions.explanation: Tutankhamun ascended the throne as a child and ruled for approximately nine years before his sudden death.

questions.text: The pharaohs were buried with 'everyday things' because:
options: They believed they could take them to the afterlife
To save space in the palace
They were gifts from the people
questions.explanation: Egyptians believed in a materialistic afterlife and provided kings with everything they might need.

questions.text: What is 'forensic reconstruction'?
options: Creating a face/body from data to see what a person looked like
questions.explanation: Using CT data, scientists can create a realistic image of how the person appeared in life.

questions.text: Who was the father or grandfather of Tutankhamun?
questions.explanation: Amenhotep III was a powerful pharaoh who ruled for almost four decades and was Tut's direct ancestor.

questions.text: The discovery of Tut's tomb was sensational because:
options: It was the first one found
It was remarkably intact despite previous looters
It contained a map of the world
It was found in the middle of a city
questions.explanation: Most royal tombs had been looted, but Tut's remained the most complete royal collection ever found.

questions.text: Aten is the:
questions.explanation: Akhenaten promoted the worship of the Aten, the sun disk, above all other gods.

questions.text: The murals in Tut's burial chamber showed:
options: Tut's journey into the afterlife with Osiris
questions.explanation: The paintings on the walls depicted the pharaoh's transition to the world of the dead.

questions.text: What is a 'sarcophagus'?
questions.explanation: A sarcophagus is the outer stone container used to hold the nested coffins.

questions.text: What was the reaction of the tourists at the tomb?
options: They were silent and respectful, some fearing the curse
They were shouting for more gold
questions.explanation: Tourists from around the world gazed at the murals in a somber, respectful silence.

questions.text: Tutankhamun was restored the 'old ways' by:
options: Reopening Amun's temples and bringing back the priests
questions.explanation: He oversaw a restoration of the traditional religion that Akhenaten had abolished.

questions.text: The CT scan created how many digital X-ray images in cross section?
questions.explanation: The scan produced 1,700 high-resolution images to capture every detail of the mummy.

questions.text: How did the Egyptian public view the CT scan?
options: With great interest and some anxiety about disturbing the dead
questions.explanation: The scanning of a national icon like King Tut was a major event followed by the public.

questions.text: The 'Valley of the Kings' is located near:
questions.explanation: It was the burial site for pharaohs and powerful nobles of the New Kingdom.

questions.text: Akhenaten was considered 'wacky' because:
options: He made sudden and extreme changes to religion and culture
questions.explanation: His religious revolution was seen as a period of strange, destabilizing change for Egypt.

questions.text: What was Tut buried with to help him in the afterlife?
options: Board games, bronze razors, and food/wine
questions.explanation: He was buried with luxury items as well as everyday essentials.

questions.text: Carter's investigation was criticized because:
options: He damaged the mummy significantly while removing it
questions.explanation: Archaeologists today criticize Carter for his unscientific and destructive methods of extracting the body.

questions.text: Amenhotep III's son was:
questions.explanation: Amenhotep IV succeeded his father and later became Akhenaten.

questions.text: The CT scan revealed a 'grey head' and 'neck vertebrae' that were:
options: As clear as in an anatomy class
questions.explanation: The scan produced surprisingly clear and detailed images of the skeletal structure.

questions.text: Who funded Howard Carter's search for Tut's tomb?
questions.explanation: Lord Carnarvon was the wealthy Englishman who financed the archaeological expeditions.

questions.text: The main theme of 'Discovering Tut' is:
options: The use of modern technology to solve historical mysteries
questions.explanation: The chapter highlights how archaeology has shifted from treasure hunting to using forensic science and technology to understand history.
